objects.name: Banking Organization (TTX participant)
objects.description: Bank subject to AML and sanctions-screening obligations across payment flows.

objects.name: AML and sanctions screening platform
objects.description: System applying watchlists, rules, and interdiction logic to customer and transaction activity.

objects.name: Tamper with compliance screening data or rules to fail open
objects.description: Adversary or error condition alters screening integrity and permits unscreened or improperly screened activity.

objects.name: Integrity failure in AML and sanctions screening
objects.description: Simulated incident where screening logic fails open and transactions proceed without proper checks.

objects.abstract: Inject 1: screening alerts unexpectedly drop to zero
objects.content: Compliance operations notices that sanctions and AML alert volumes have dropped abnormally during a period of normal transaction throughput.

objects.abstract: Inject 2: retrospective review and regulator call
objects.content: The bank must decide whether to halt certain payment classes, reconstruct the affected transaction population, and escalate immediately to compliance leadership and regulators.

objects.name: Integrity monitoring for compliance rules and watchlists
objects.description: Continuously validate rule-set integrity, watchlist updates, and fail-open conditions for compliance systems.

objects.name: Retrospective transaction screening and escalation procedure
objects.description: Define hold, replay, lookback, and reporting steps for periods where compliance controls may have failed.

objects.name: TTX Scenario 10 - Data integrity attack on AML and sanctions screening
objects.description: Scenario tests integrity assurance, compensating controls, and regulatory escalation when screening controls fail.
